Environmental Impact Statement, Federal Aid Route 77, Illinois Route 59, E.W. Tollway to Illinois 64, DuPage County, Illinois

The project involves widening Illinois Route 59 from two to four lanes, stretching from the East-West Tollway to Illinois Route 64 in DuPage County, Illinois. This 7.81-mile improvement aims to accommodate increasing traffic due to urbanization and nearby developments like the National Accelerator Laboratory. The plan includes a grade separation at the Illinois Prairie Path to ensure safety for pedestrians and equestrians. While the project is expected to displace six homes and possibly one business, it promises to alleviate congestion and improve traffic flow, with minimal environmental impact.

The widening of Illinois Route 59 was proposed to address the growing traffic demands due to urbanization and nearby developments, including the National Accelerator Laboratory. There was some public concern regarding the displacement of homes and businesses, but the project was seen as necessary for improving traffic flow and safety. Ultimately, the project aimed to balance infrastructure needs with environmental considerations.

"The provision of an underpass structure at the Prairie Path crossing is deemed to be important and desirable for the safety of both highway and path users, and the Board approves of this concept." — E.M. Lewis, Secretary, Illinois Prairie Path Board
"We strongly urge that this much needed improvement be expedited in every possible way." — Ronald A. Dold, Superintendent of Highways, DuPage County Highway Department
